signature: Open dynamical systems: expanding maps/shifts with holes — survivor sets, escape rates, dimension drop and its phase transitions in hole position/size. Instantiation hunted: accelerated Collatz T on Z_2 is conjugate to the full 2-shift via parity itineraries; convergence to 1 = absorption by a hole anchored at the period-2 point 1<->2; divergent integers = N-points of a survivor set; DFA divergence certificates = subshifts avoiding the hole.

finds.ref: Jian Lu, Wolfgang Steiner, Yuru Zou, "Hausdorff dimension of double base expansions and binary shifts with a hole", 2025, arXiv:2509.04227
finds.the_theorem: Setup: two bases q0,q1>1; pi_{q0,q1}(i1 i2 ...) = sum_k i_k/(q_{i1}...q_{ik}), attractor of IFS {x/q0, (x+1)/q1}. Hole = lexicographic interval ]a,b[ in {0,1}^inf (a in 0{0,1}^inf, b in 1{0,1}^inf); survivor set Omega_{a,b} = {sequences no tail of which falls strictly between a and b} — a closed subshift. Theorem 1.2: for admissible pairs (a,b), either pi_{beta,beta}(a) < pi_{beta,beta}(b) for all beta>1 and then h(Omega_{a,b}) = 0 = dim, OR h(Omega_{a,b}) = log beta* where beta* solves pi_{beta,beta}(a) = pi_{beta,beta}(b), and dim_H pi_{q0,q1}(Omega_{a,b}) = min{1,s} with s solving pi_{q0^s,q1^s}(a) = pi_{q0^s,q1^s}(b). Theorem 1.1: dim_H of the univoque set U_{q0,q1} is 0 / in (0,1) via the same equation / =1, according to thresholds K(q0) and q0/(q0-1). Continuity in (q0,q1) (Thms 1.4-1.5).
finds.the_match: This is the strongest known structure theorem for the full 2-shift with a (lexicographic, hence in particular cylinder) hole — exactly our symbolic setting. The two-base weighting is the Collatz-natural one: taking q0=2, q1=3/2 makes pi_{q0^s,q1^s} the Lyapunov-weighted dimension for the two slopes (2 and 3/2) of accelerated Collatz, so any lexicographic-hole constraint we derive on parity vectors gets an EXACT entropy and an exact Collatz-weighted dimension from one transcendental equation. GAPS, flagged: (i) the IFS offsets ((x+1)/q1 vs Collatz's (3x+1)/2 inverse branch (2y-1)/3) differ, so their geometric attractor J_{q0,q1} is not the Collatz real line or Z_2 — only the symbolic Theorem 1.2 transfers verbatim; (ii) the Collatz absorption region (basin of 1) is not a single lexicographic interval, so one must first derive a lexicographic/one-sided hole condition satisfied by divergent itineraries (see the KKLL find for how); (iii) their theory says nothing about whether the countable arithmetic set N meets the survivor set — it controls all survivors, not integer ones.
finds.transfer: Compute h and the (log2, log3/2)-weighted dimension of the divergence subshift Sigma_delta = {omega : all partial sums of (i_j log3 - log2 + delta) >= 0} by exhibiting it as Omega_{a,b} for explicit boundary words a,b, via the equation pi_{beta,beta}(a) = pi_{beta,beta}(b); identify the lexicographically extremal admissible divergent itineraries as the boundary words and compute their 2-adic points x(omega) mod 2^k to extract new congruence constraints on hypothetical divergent orbits beyond the density bound log2/log3.

finds.ref: Claudio Bonanno, Giampaolo Cristadoro, Marco Lenci, "Maximal escape rate for shifts", 2022, Discrete and Continuous Dynamical Systems 42(12); arXiv:2112.14248
finds.the_theorem: Full shift on A^N with Bernoulli product measure (p_a > 0), hole = cylinder [w], |w| = r. Proposition 2.4: the escape rate is gamma_w = log z0, z0 the smallest positive root of tau_w(z) = mu(w) z^r + (1-z) c_w(p,z), where c_w is the weighted autocorrelation polynomial of w (Guibas-Odlyzko correlations weighted by symbol probabilities). Theorems 3.4-3.5: the maximal-escape-rate hole of given length is one of two explicit words, determined by the most probable symbol; for unequal p the escape-rate ordering DIFFERS from both the measure ordering and ordering by shortest periodic orbit in the hole.
finds.the_match: Exact match, no gaps in the mechanism: Haar measure on Z_2 = Bernoulli(1/2,1/2) on parity itineraries; the ball 1+2^N Z_2 around the trivial cycle is the cylinder w = (10)^{N/2}; balls around the other integer cycles of T on Z are 0^N (fixed point 0), 1^N (fixed point -1), (110)^{N/3} (the -5,-7,-10 cycle). tau_w is explicitly solvable for each: e.g. for w=(10)^{N/2}, c_w(z) = sum_{j even < N} (z/2)^j. The interpretive gap: escape rate is w.r.t. Haar on Z_2, while our real interest is counting absorption of N-points — equating the two is the standard (unproven) equidistribution heuristic; also a divergent orbit need not avoid any fixed ball around 1, so this controls absorption statistics, not divergence itself. The biased-p case (p_1 = log2/log3, the tilted ensemble forced on divergent orbits by the drift condition) is exactly their 'unequal probabilities' regime where the ordering reshuffles.
finds.transfer: Compute exact escape rates gamma_w for the cylinder holes around each known cycle at p = (1/2,1/2), and prediction curves 1 - lambda(Omega_n) for the fraction of residues mod 2^N not yet absorbed after n steps; test against Barina-style verification statistics (stopping-time histograms up to 2^71). Then recompute at the tilted p_1 = log2/log3 to rank which cycle neighborhood is the slowest trap in the ensemble a divergent orbit must inhabit.

finds.ref: Leonid A. Bunimovich, Alex Yurchenko, "Where to place a hole to achieve a maximal escape rate", 2011, Israel Journal of Mathematics 182; arXiv:0811.4438
finds.the_theorem: Doubling map, Markov (dyadic) holes of equal size 2^{-N}. Theorem 4.5.3: if the minimal period tau of periodic orbits meeting hole I_j exceeds that of I_i, then escape rate rho(I_j) > rho(I_i); moreover the finite-time survivor measures are ordered for ALL n >= tau(I_i), not just asymptotically. Theorem 4.6.1: local escape rate at a period-m point x: lim_N rho(I_N(x))/lambda(I_N(x)) = 1 - 2^{-m}; at nonperiodic points the limit is 1. Theorem 4.7.2: max over holes of size 2^{-(N+1)} of rho equals min over holes of size 2^{-N}. Proof mechanism: overlap/autocorrelation of the coding word — c_{l+1}=1 iff the hole cylinder contains a period-l point.
finds.the_match: Instantiates verbatim on Z_2 (conjugate to the doubling map picture): dyadic holes = residue classes mod 2^N. The itineraries of the known 3x+1 integer cycles occupy precisely the extremal slow-escape hole positions: 0^N and 1^N (fixed points 0 and -1, slowest), (10)^{N/2} (trivial cycle 1<->2, period 2, next slowest), (110)^{N/3} (the -5 cycle, period 3). So 'the trivial cycle is the worst-placed hole among all positions not containing a fixed point' is a theorem-level statement, valid at all finite times — important because verification data is finite-time. Gap: same as BCL — this is measure-theoretic escape on Z_2; transferring to counting integers below X uses the unproven equidistribution of N in Z_2 under T, and divergence itself is untouched (point holes have zero measure).
finds.transfer: State and prove: among all residue classes mod 2^N of equal size, absorption into the trivial cycle's class is slower than into any class not meeting a cycle of period <= 2, with the explicit finite-time ordering of Thm 4.5.3; use it to derive rigorous two-sided bounds on the count of n <= X with stopping time > k, and compare against the verification record to quantify how 'anomalously slow' observed convergence is.

finds.ref: Gerhard Keller, Carlangelo Liverani, "Rare events, escape rates and quasistationarity: some exact formulae", 2009, J. Stat. Phys. 135; arXiv:0810.2229
finds.the_theorem: Abstract perturbation Theorem 2.1 for families of transfer operators P_eps (assumptions A1-A6: uniform Lasota-Yorke inequalities plus a rare-perturbation smallness condition) giving first-order expansion of the leading eigenvalue. Corollary for the doubling map (their formula 1.1): if z is a period-p point and I_eps a hole of length eps containing z, the survival eigenvalue expands as lambda_eps = 1 - eps(1 - 2^{-p}) + o(eps). Explicitly extended to unions of intervals, Gauss map, coin-tossing stopped at first occurrence of a pattern from a finite collection, and — their Section 5 — the Shannon capacity of constrained binary codes.
finds.the_match: Two precise instantiations. (1) Hole shrinking to the trivial cycle (p=2): escape rate ~ (3/4) mu(hole), vs ~ mu(hole) at generic positions — the exact derivative of absorption speed at the Collatz hole. (2) Their Section 5 identifies escape rates of shifts with forbidden words with capacities of constrained coding systems: our proved result 'no regular divergence certificate with <=7 DFA states in base 2' lives exactly in that constrained-systems category, so KL's eigenvalue-perturbation technology is the analytic counterpart of our automata search. Gap: their theorems need holes of positive measure with uniform Lasota-Yorke control; the Collatz point-hole {1} is a measure-zero limit, so only the eps->0 asymptotics transfer, and again only for absorption statistics on Z_2, not for the arithmetic divergence question.
finds.transfer: Rigorous escape-rate asymptotics as the home for absorption statistics: prove lambda(2^{-N} ball at 1) = 1 - (3/4) 2^{-N} + o(2^{-N}) on Z_2 and match the measured decay of unconverged integers in verification data; separately, recast the DFA-certificate non-existence as a capacity computation for the constrained system avoiding the basin words and push it past 7 states with transfer-operator (rather than enumeration) methods.

finds.ref: Charlene Kalle, Derong Kong, Niels Langeveld, Wenxia Li, "The beta-transformation with a hole at 0", 2020, Ergodic Theory Dynam. Systems 40; arXiv:1803.07338 — together with Pieter Allaart, Derong Kong, "Entropy plateaus..." arXiv:2304.06892, and Yun Sun, Bing Li, Yiming Ding, "Topological expansive Lorenz maps with a hole at critical point", 2023, arXiv:2311.02465
finds.the_theorem: For T_beta(x) = beta x mod 1, beta in (1,2], survivor set K_beta(t) = {x : T_beta^n(x) not in (0,t) for all n} (orbit never falls below t). Theorems: there is a critical tau_beta <= 1 - 1/beta with dim_H K_beta(t) > 0 iff t < tau_beta; the bifurcation set E_beta (where t -> K_beta(t) is not locally constant) is Lebesgue-null with full Hausdorff dimension; the dimension/entropy function of t is a devil's staircase with explicitly characterized plateaus (Allaart-Kong). Sun-Li-Ding extend the devil's staircase entropy and (via Ledrappier-Young) dimension results to general topological expansive Lorenz maps (two slopes allowed) with the hole at the critical point, with survivor sets characterized symbolically up to countable sets by kneading data of an associated Lorenz map.
finds.the_match: This is the deepest structural match found. Derivation (ours, elementary): a divergent orbit attains its minimum n_min > 2^71 (Barina verification); from the minimum onward, log n_{m+k} - log n_m >= 0 for all k, and the itinerary walk S_k = sum_{j<k} (i_j log 3 - log 2) satisfies S_k >= -E_k with 0 <= E_k <= k/(3 n_min). Hence the tail itinerary lies in the uniform one-sided survivor set Sigma_delta = {omega : S_k(omega) >= -delta k for all k}, delta = 1/(3*2^71) — exactly a 'hole at 0' survivor condition for the two-slope (log 2, log 3/2) system, i.e. the object whose entropy/dimension in t is the KKLL/Allaart-Kong devil's staircase and whose two-slope version is Sun-Li-Ding's theorem. The plateau/bifurcation machinery characterizes the lexicographically extremal surviving itineraries; at critical (zero-drift) parameter these are rotation-word (Sturmian-type) sequences of slope log2/log3 — the same continued-fraction convergents of log2/log3 (5/8, 12/19, 53/84, ...) that drive Simons-de Weger cycle-nonexistence bounds, now appearing for DIVERGENCE. GAPS, flagged honestly: (i) divergence gives the uniform condition only for the tail from the orbit minimum and only with the delta-error (the naive liminf-density constraint is NOT a survivor condition — see dead ends); (ii) the published theorems are for interval maps T_beta / Lorenz maps, while Sigma_delta is a walk-on-R (skew product) survivor set — the symbolic kneading characterization is what transfers, and the two-slope case needs Sun-Li-Ding rather than KKLL; (iii) the Sturmian identification of plateau endpoints is documented for the doubling/Lorenz families (Glendinning-Sidorov's rotation-number renormalization; classical Urbanski 1986/87 devil's staircase for {x : orbit >= t} — verified here only through secondary citations, not the original paper); (iv) as always, positive dimension of Sigma_delta says nothing about it meeting N.
finds.transfer: Prove the half-page lemma 'every divergent 3x+1 orbit has a tail itinerary in Sigma_delta, delta = 1/(3*2^71)'; then run the Allaart-Kong/Sun-Li-Ding plateau machinery on the two-slope system to (a) compute h(Sigma_delta) exactly and (b) characterize its lexicographically minimal elements as explicit rotation words of slope log2/log3; finally compute the 2-adic points x(omega) of those extremal words mod 2^k, yielding congruence classes mod 2^k that any near-critical divergent orbit must enter — a constraint strictly finer than the density bound liminf >= log2/log3.

finds.ref: Paul Glendinning, Nikita Sidorov, "The doubling map with asymmetrical holes", 2015, Ergodic Theory Dynam. Systems 35; arXiv:1302.2486
finds.the_theorem: For the doubling map with hole (a,b), 0 < a < 1/2 < b < 1, survivor set J(a,b) = {x : T^n x not in (a,b) for all n >= 0}. Complete characterizations: J(a,b) is infinite in every neighborhood-sense iff b <= phi(a) (Thm 2.7, phi explicit); dim_H J(a,b) > 0 iff b < chi(a) (Thm 1.2/2.13, chi explicit via renormalization with rotation numbers); dim_H J(a,b) > 0 whenever b - a < (1/4) prod_{n>=1} (1 - 2^{-2^n}) ~ 0.175092, and this constant is sharp. Machinery: extremal pairs (s,t) of words with s1=0, t1=1 satisfying the two-sided lexicographic conditions s^inf <= sigma^k s^inf < t^inf and s^inf < sigma^l t^inf <= t^inf — the 'lexicographic world'.
finds.the_match: The extremal-pair inequalities (2.1) are precisely the shape of a shift-invariant compact set avoiding a region, i.e. the shape of our divergence certificates (invariant sets of the transduction avoiding 1): every certificate subshift is pinned between such extremal words. Their phase boundary separates holes admitting only countable/zero-entropy survivors (where all survivors are forced to be eventually periodic rotation words) from holes with positive-dimension survivor sets. Gap: our proved certificate obstruction is about REGULAR (sofic) invariant sets in base-2 integer representation, an arithmetic category; the GS dichotomy concerns all closed invariant sets for a geometric hole, and the basin of 1 is not a single interval (a,b) in any obvious coordinate — the transfer needs the basin's cylinder skeleton approximated by lexicographic holes from inside.
finds.transfer: For the inner lexicographic approximations of the Collatz absorption region, decide which side of the chi-boundary they fall on: if inside the zero-entropy regime, every closed invariant set avoiding that approximation consists of rotation words only — which would upgrade our '<=7-state DFA' certificate obstruction to 'any certificate subshift must contain rotation words of slope log2/log3', connecting certificate non-existence to cycle non-existence.

finds.ref: Derong Kong, Beibei Sun, Zhiqiang Wang, "Open dynamical systems with a moving hole", 2025, arXiv:2505.02336
finds.the_theorem: For T_b(x) = bx mod 1, b >= 3 integer, and a moving hole: at time n the hole is the b-adic cylinder I_{sigma^n omega} determined by shifting a fixed sequence omega; survivor set K^omega = {x : T_b^n x not in I_{sigma^n omega} for all n >= 0}. Theorems: dim_H K^omega = lower box dimension and dim_P = upper box dimension; sharp explicitly computable bounds on these; for every admissible pair alpha <= beta there exist (infinitely many) omega with dim_H K^omega = alpha and dim_P K^omega = beta. Applications to badly approximable numbers and joint spectral radius.
finds.the_match: The only survivor-set framework found that allows the hole to change every step — the structural signature of the BB cryptids: Antihydra/Hydra halting is 'the counter functional of the itinerary hits an exact boundary', i.e. the orbit of the skew product (parity shift) x (counter walk on Z) enters the time-dependent set {counter = 0}; symbolically, the surviving (non-halting) itineraries form the survivor set of a history-dependent hole. GAPS, serious and flagged: (i) their hole sequence is exogenous (shift of a fixed omega, known in advance), while the cryptid hole is endogenous (a functional of the point's own past) — the survivor set is then not of their product form; (ii) b >= 3 excludes the binary shift, so even the exogenous version needs reworking for alphabet {0,1}; (iii) their measure-zero/dimension results again say nothing about the single arithmetic orbit the cryptid actually runs. Treat as a template for 'dimension theory of moving-hole survivor sets', not as an off-the-shelf theorem.
finds.transfer: Formulate the Antihydra non-halting set as the survivor set of a skew-product hole {(omega, c) : c = 0} over the full 2-shift with fiber walk steps +2/-1, prove a Kong-Sun-Wang-style dimension formula for the set of surviving itineraries, and derive the (1/phi)^h escape heuristic as the exact fiber escape rate (gambler's ruin eigenvalue) — giving the cryptid heuristic a theorem-shaped statement whose only unproved input is equidistribution of the specific orbit.

best_single_bet: Play out the KKLL/Allaart-Kong/Sun-Li-Ding 'hole at 0' transfer (find 5, fed into Lu-Steiner-Zou's Theorem 1.2 for exact entropy/dimension). First move: write and verify the half-page lemma that every divergent 3x+1 orbit has a tail itinerary in Sigma_delta = {omega : sum_{j<k}(i_j log 3 - log 2) >= -delta k for all k} with delta = 1/(3*2^71) (orbit-minimum argument + Barina's n_min > 2^71), then compute the lexicographically minimal words of Sigma_delta via the two-slope kneading/plateau machinery and their 2-adic points mod 2^k — the deliverable is congruence constraints on near-critical divergent orbits strictly beyond the log2/log3 density bound, with the Sturmian slope-log2/log3 boundary words tying divergence to the same CF convergents (5/8, 12/19, 53/84) that govern Simons-de Weger cycle bounds.
dead_ends: (1) The naive point-hole framing is vacuous: in Z_2 the basin of the trivial cycle is countable (T^{-k}{1} has exactly 2^k points), so the survivor set of the hole {1} is co-countable with full dimension — survivor-set dimension theory says nothing there; content only appears for positive-size (cylinder) holes. (2) Ball-hole survivor sets do NOT sandwich the divergent set: a divergent orbit may enter 1 + 2^m Z_2 infinitely often at large values, so 'divergent implies survives the ball hole at 1' is false for every fixed m — do not re-attempt transfer of ball-survivor dimension bounds directly to divergent orbits (the orbit-minimum/one-sided-walk reformulation in find 5 is the fix). (3) The raw liminf-density constraint on divergent parity vectors is an eventual/limsup condition, not a uniform survivor condition — hole theorems don't apply to it as-is. (4) Hensley restricted-digit continued fractions (and the Gauss map with holes generally): adjacent, but no direct Collatz instantiation beyond what the 2-shift versions already give; the reusable part is only the rigorous transfer-operator dimension technology (Jenkinson-Pollicott 1611.09276, downloaded as a computational tool for certificate-subshift dimensions). (5) arXiv:1201.6480 is a false hit (a paper on Seiffert means, not Glendinning-Sidorov — correct id is 1302.2486). (6) Every paper checked (all ten downloaded PDFs, grepped full text) is Collatz-blind — zero mentions of Collatz/3x+1/3n+1 — so no hidden prior art was found in this corner; nobody in the maps-with-holes school appears to have noticed that their hole-position extremality theorems place the 3x+1 integer cycles at exactly the slowest-escape hole positions.
